title: Coelogyne apinnata
intro: Coelogyne apinnata, also known as the Himalayan orchid, is a species of orchid native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia, characterized by its unique and fragrant flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne apinnata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is also used in the production of herbal teas and capsules, making it a valuable resource for natural health remedies.
taxonomy: Coelogyne apinnata is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. This epiphytic orchid is native to tropical Asia and is classified under the genus Coelogyne. The specific classification of Coelogyne apinnata is within the tribe Coelogyneae. It belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae. The family Orchidaceae is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants.
